ucs.title: [LLM] Higaisa LNK-borne spearphish: cmd.exe carving embedded payload from .lnk via findstr/certutil with decoy PDF drop
ucs.description: Hunts Higaisa's signature initial-access chain in which a spearphished ZIP contains a double-extension .pdf.lnk. When opened, the LNK invokes cmd.exe to self-extract an embedded base64/PE blob from the same .lnk file using findstr/certutil, then opens a decoy PDF to mask execution. This pattern was the core delivery TTP across the 2020 Malwarebytes/Zscaler/PT Security disclosures of G0126.
ucs.splunk: | tstats summariesonly=t count min(_time) as firstTime max(_time) as lastTime values(Processes.process) as process values(Processes.process_name) as process_name values(Processes.parent_process) as parent_cmdline values(Processes.user) as user from datamodel=Endpoint.Processes where Processes.parent_process_name IN ("explorer.exe","OUTLOOK.EXE","WINWORD.EXE") AND Processes.process_name="cmd.exe" AND (Processes.process="*findstr*\".lnk*" OR Processes.process="*for /f*.lnk*" OR (Processes.process="*certutil*-decode*" AND Processes.process="*.lnk*") OR Processes.process="*start /min*") by Processes.dest Processes.parent_process_id Processes.process_id _time | `drop_dm_object_name(Processes)` | join type=inner dest [| tstats summariesonly=t count from datamodel=Endpoint.Filesystem where Filesystem.file_name="*.pdf" AND Filesystem.file_path IN ("*\\Temp\\*","*\\AppData\\Local\\Temp\\*","*\\Public\\*") by Filesystem.dest Filesystem.file_name Filesystem.file_path | `drop_dm_object_name(Filesystem)` | rename file_name as decoy_pdf] | where lastTime - firstTime < 120 | table firstTime dest user parent_cmdline process decoy_pdf
ucs.kql:
let lnkCarvers = DeviceProcessEvents
| where Timestamp > ago(7d)
| where InitiatingProcessFileName in~ ("explorer.exe","outlook.exe","winword.exe","7zfm.exe","winrar.exe")
| where FileName =~ "cmd.exe"
| where ProcessCommandLine has ".lnk"
    and (ProcessCommandLine has_any ("findstr","for /f","certutil -decode","certutil /decode")
         or ProcessCommandLine has_all ("start","/min"))
| project carveTime=Timestamp, DeviceId, DeviceName, AccountName, lnkCmd=ProcessCommandLine, ParentImg=InitiatingProcessFileName, ParentCmd=InitiatingProcessCommandLine;
let decoyDrops = DeviceFileEvents
| where Timestamp > ago(7d)
| where ActionType == "FileCreated"
| where FileName endswith ".pdf"
| where FolderPath has_any (@"\Temp\", @"\AppData\Local\Temp\", @"\Public\")
| project dropTime=Timestamp, DeviceId, decoyPath=FolderPath, decoyName=FileName, droppingProcess=InitiatingProcessFileName;
lnkCarvers
| join kind=inner decoyDrops on DeviceId
| where (dropTime - carveTime) between (-2m .. 2m)
| project carveTime, DeviceName, AccountName, ParentImg, lnkCmd, decoyName, decoyPath
ucs.rationale: Generic LNK-execution alerts are noisy. Higaisa's actual operational pattern is uniquely narrow: a user-launched LNK spawns cmd.exe which references the LNK file by name via findstr/certutil to carve an embedded payload, with a near-simultaneous PDF decoy drop in Temp/Public. Requiring the LNK-as-data-source string AND a decoy PDF write within ~120s on the same host targets G0126's documented deli

ucs.title: [LLM] Higaisa scheduled-task persistence: schtasks creating recurring jobs that launch wscript/mshta with hidden windows from %AppData%/%Public%
ucs.description: Hunts Higaisa's post-exploitation persistence: after the LNK detonates, the actor establishes a scheduled task that re-launches a JavaScript/VBScript or HTA stager from a user-writable path with /min or hidden-window flags. This combo of T1053.005 + T1059.005/.007 + T1564.003 + T1220 targeting %AppData%/%Public% paths is consistently observed across G0126 intrusions.
ucs.splunk: | tstats summariesonly=t count min(_time) as firstTime max(_time) as lastTime values(Processes.process) as cmd values(Processes.parent_process_name) as parent values(Processes.user) as user from datamodel=Endpoint.Processes where Processes.process_name="schtasks.exe" AND Processes.process="*/create*" AND (Processes.process="*wscript*" OR Processes.process="*cscript*" OR Processes.process="*mshta*" OR Processes.process="*wmic*os*get*/format*") AND (Processes.process="*\\AppData\\*" OR Processes.process="*\\Public\\*" OR Processes.process="*\\ProgramData\\*" OR Processes.process="*\\Users\\Public\\*") by Processes.dest Processes.user Processes.parent_process_name | `drop_dm_object_name(Processes)` | rex field=cmd "/tn\s+\"?(?<task_name>[^\"\s]+)" | rex field=cmd "/tr\s+\"?(?<task_run>[^\"]+)" | where NOT match(parent,"(?i)(SCCM|ccmexec|gpscript|TiWorker)\.exe") | table firstTime dest user parent task_name task_run cmd
ucs.kql:
DeviceProcessEvents
| where Timestamp > ago(14d)
| where FileName =~ "schtasks.exe"
| where ProcessCommandLine has_cs "/create"
| where ProcessCommandLine has_any ("wscript","cscript","mshta","wmic os get /format")
| where ProcessCommandLine has_any (@"\AppData\", @"\Public\", @"\ProgramData\", @"\Users\Public\")
| extend taskName = extract(@"/tn\s+\"?([^\"\s]+)", 1, ProcessCommandLine)
| extend taskRun  = extract(@"/tr\s+\"?([^\"]+?)\"?(?:\s+/|$)", 1, ProcessCommandLine)
| where InitiatingProcessFileName !in~ ("ccmexec.exe","gpscript.exe","tiworker.exe")
| join kind=leftouter (
    DeviceProcessEvents
    | where Timestamp > ago(14d)
    | where FileName in~ ("wscript.exe","cscript.exe","mshta.exe","wmic.exe")
    | where InitiatingProcessFileName =~ "svchost.exe" and InitiatingProcessCommandLine has "Schedule"
    | project DeviceId, scriptExec=Timestamp, scriptCmd=ProcessCommandLine
) on DeviceId
| where isnull(scriptExec) or scriptExec > Timestamp
| project Timestamp, DeviceName, AccountName, taskName, taskRun, ProcessCommandLine, scriptCmd
ucs.rationale: Most enterprise scheduled tasks point at signed binaries in Program Files; Higaisa's chain instead writes its stager to AppData/Public/ProgramData and reanimates it via wscript/cscript/mshta or the WMIC XSL trick (T1220) with hidden-window flags. Joining task creation to subsequent svchost->wscript/mshta children initiated by the Schedule service confirms the persistence executed and reduces FPs v
